This volume is a comprehensive continuation and extension of a previous edition published
in 2015 and presents the latest developments of the main pillars of protein analysis, namely
sample preparation, separation, and characterization Individual technologies of each pillar
combined into complementary and robust workﬂows render proteomic analysis of complex
biological samples even more powerful and are the prerequisite to gain maximum value from
biological samples in a single experiment Various modern mass spectrometry techniques have become indispensable tools in
proteome research, and their importance is very well reﬂected throughout this book in
general and by the ﬁrst chapter of this volume in particular Rudolf Grimm provides a highly
inspiring story about the ﬁrst complex multi-omics study of the oldest human ice mummy,
the 5300-year-old Iceman or Oetzi, and how mass spectrometry was applied Core areas in
this volume are protocols for the analysis of post-translational modiﬁcations and protein
interaction partners, followed by sophisticated procedures to enrich for extracellular vesicles
and organelles In addition, several types of protein immuno-assays are described and
complemented by various methods for the characterization of antibodies and host-cell
protein analysis Last but not least, a few standard sample preparation protocols and recent
advances concerning immuno-chemical detection of proteins are included as well Special thanks to the authors of the individual chapters who are well-known protein
biochemists, and all of them have made the valuable choice to provide a detailed representa-
tion of their lab work and to share important tips and tricks for a successful and reproducible
employment of their precious protocols in other laboratories This book is for students of Biochemistry, Biomedicine, Biology, and Genomics and will
be an invaluable source for the experienced, practicing scientist, too Munich, Germany Anton Posch

summary: Proteomic Profiling, Second Edition, edited by Anton Posch, provides comprehensive and reliable step-by-step protocols for protein analysis in the field of Molecular Biology. This collaboration between the School of Life and Medical Sciences at the University of Hertfordshire and Bio-Rad Laboratories GmbH covers sample preparation, separation, and characterization using modern mass spectrometry techniques. The book includes protocols for the analysis of post-translational modifications, protein interaction partners, extracellular vesicles, organelles, and protein immuno-assays. It also features procedures for antibody characterization, host-cell protein analysis, and standard sample preparation. With a focus on reproducibility and indexing in PubMed, this publication is a valuable resource for protein biochemists, experienced scientists, and students in the fields of biochemistry, biomedicine, biology, and genomics.

topics.justification: The book focuses on providing protocols for protein analysis, making proteomic profiling a central theme.
The book provides step-by-step protocols for protein analysis, covering sample preparation, separation, and characterization.
The book emphasizes the use of modern mass spectrometry techniques for protein analysis.
The book includes protocols for the analysis of post-translational modifications of proteins.
The book features protocols for the analysis of protein interaction partners.
The book covers protocols for the analysis of extracellular vesicles and organelles, which are important in protein analysis.
The book includes protocols for protein immuno-assays, which are used to detect and quantify proteins.
The book features procedures for antibody characterization, an important aspect of protein analysis.
The book includes protocols for the analysis of host-cell proteins, which are relevant in protein analysis.
The book provides protocols for standard sample preparation, an essential step in protein analysis.
